name: Punta Sur
region: North America, Mexico, Yucatan Peninsula, Quintana Roo, Cozumel
description: A drift dive along Cozumel's southern tip, where strong currents sweep you past dramatic walls and swim-throughs. Eagle rays and turtles glide effortlessly in the blue, while vibrant coral formations teem with tropical fish. The steep drop-offs and caverns create thrilling terrain, with occasional nurse sharks resting in the shadows. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, making it a photographer's dream. Best suited for intermediate to advanced divers due to the currents.

name: Cedral Wall
region: North America, Mexico, Yucatan Peninsula, Quintana Roo, Cozumel
description: A steep wall dive with dramatic coral formations and abundant marine life. The current sweeps you along as you drift past sponges, sea fans, and overhangs. Look for turtles, nurse sharks, and eagle rays gliding by. Depth ranges from 40 to 100 feet, with excellent visibility. Perfect for intermediate divers who enjoy drift diving. The vibrant reef teems with tropical fish, making it a colorful underwater spectacle.

name: San Juan
region: North America, Mexico, Yucatan Peninsula, Quintana Roo, Cozumel
description: This site off Cozumel features dramatic walls and swim-throughs covered in vibrant sponges and corals. Strong currents attract large schools of fish, including jacks and barracuda. Eagle rays and turtles are common. The steep drop-off plunges into deep blue, offering thrilling drift dives. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. Best for advanced divers due to currents.

name: Punta Molas
region: North America, Mexico, Yucatan Peninsula, Quintana Roo, Cozumel
description: Punta Molas sits on Cozumel's northern tip, where strong currents attract pelagic life like eagle rays and sharks. The steep wall drops to over 100 feet, covered in vibrant sponges and black coral. Drift diving here offers thrilling rides along the reef, with occasional sightings of loggerheads and hawksbill turtles. Visibility often exceeds 100 feet, revealing dramatic overhangs and swim-throughs. The site suits experienced divers due to unpredictable currents. Expect encounters with large schools of horse-eye jacks and the occasional bull shark. The remote location means fewer crowds and pristine conditions.

name: Yellow House
region: North America, Mexico, Yucatan Peninsula, Quintana Roo, Cozumel
description: This dive site off Cozumel features a stunning coral-covered pinnacle rising from the sandy bottom, attracting schools of grunts, snappers, and the occasional eagle ray. The current can be strong, making it ideal for drift diving. Vibrant sponges and sea fans adorn the walls, while turtles and nurse sharks are common sightings. Depths range from 40 to 100 feet, offering something for all experience divers. The clear Caribbean waters provide excellent visibility year-round.

name: Palancar: Gardens
region: North America, Mexico, Yucatan Peninsula, Quintana Roo, Cozumel
description: This shallow reef in Cozumel features vibrant coral gardens teeming with tropical fish, sponges, and sea fans. Gentle currents make it ideal for beginners, while the intricate coral formations and abundant marine life keep experienced divers engaged. The clear Caribbean waters offer excellent visibility, often exceeding 30 meters. Expect encounters with parrotfish, angelfish, and occasional eagle rays gliding over the reef. The site's easy access and calm conditions make it perfect for relaxed exploration.

name: Barracuda Pinnacles
region: North America, Mexico, Yucatan Peninsula, Quintana Roo, Cozumel
description: A stunning dive site off Cozumel featuring dramatic pinnacles rising from the seabed, covered in vibrant coral formations. Schools of barracuda patrol the area, along with turtles, eagle rays, and occasional reef sharks. Strong currents create drift diving conditions, perfect for experienced divers. The site offers excellent visibility and depths ranging from 15 to 30 meters, with swim-throughs and overhangs adding excitement. The pinnacles attract abundant marine life, making it a must-dive for underwater photographers.

name: Colombia Deep
region: North America, Mexico, Yucatan Peninsula, Quintana Roo, Cozumel
description: Dropping to 100 feet, this dramatic wall dive near Cozumel features towering coral formations and abundant marine life. Strong currents attract large pelagics like eagle rays and sharks, while the steep wall is covered in colorful sponges and black coral. Visibility often exceeds 100 feet, making it ideal for drift diving. The depth and current make this site best for advanced divers.
